overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a strong, highly targeted academic presence across Bluesky, Mastodon, and LinkedIn, focusing on computational social science, disinformation, and coordinated behavior. With a total following of 3,580, LinkedIn serves as the largest audience hub, while Bluesky yields the highest relative engagement per post, indicating a highly active and receptive network for research-centric updates.

contentThemes.description: Updates on research publications, tools like CooRnet v2.0, and presentations detailing computational methods to detect coordinated link sharing.
Call-outs for postdoctoral positions and PhD opportunities within the computational social science space.
Announcements of new grants and projects dedicated to investigating information operations.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high engagement rate to share early-stage research ideas, interactive questions, and call-outs for academic collaborations.
Utilize LinkedIn's larger audience by incorporating visual assets (infographics, photos, or PDF slides) to further boost engagement on funding and recruitment milestones.
Cross-promote software releases and methodological discussions on Mastodon to tap deeper into its specialized academic community (aoir.social).
Establish a more consistent posting schedule across all platforms to maintain audience momentum.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky is the most engaging platform with an average engagement rate of 6.14% per post. It shows a highly responsive audience particularly interested in direct research announcements and student opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides steady engagement (4.55% average rate) from a niche, decentralized academic network, proving highly receptive to technical tool releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0) and methodological discussions.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn represents the largest audience hub (1,850 followers) and yields the highest absolute interactions, making it highly effective for professional announcements, grant wins, and recruitment.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
